data.title: Saudi Arabia lowers July oil prices for Asia after OPEC+ supply boost
data.description:
Key points:
  • July Arab Light OSP falls by 20 cents/bbl
  • Price cut smaller than expected
  • Higher Saudi demand may limit export, analyst says

Saudi Arabia, the world's biggest oil exporter, on Wednesday lowered its July prices for Asian buyers after OPEC+ hiked output for a fourth month although the price cut was smaller than expected.

Saudi Arabia's state firm Aramco cut the official selling price for the flagship Arab light crude it sells to Asia for July to $1.20 a barrel above the Oman/Dubai average, 20 cents lower than June and the lowest since May.

Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allies such as Russia are raising output. Eight OPEC+ countries on Saturday agreed to another big increase of 411,000 bpd for July, having increased output by the same amount in May and June.

However, Saudi Arabia's 20-cent price cut was smaller than the 40-cent to 50-cent reduction expected in a Reuters survey.

"A smaller reduction was likely due to strong domestic crude burn in Saudi Arabia and refinery runs that could limit barrels available for export," said Richard Jones, an analyst at consultancy Energy Aspects.

The Middle East typically burns crude and high-sulphur fuel oil for power between June and August, for the peak demand season. Analysts expected Saudi to burn more crude oil for power generation this summer.

Saudi crude OSPs set the price trend for other grades exported by Iran, Kuwait and Iraq, affecting about 9 million barrels per day of crude bound for Asia.

The tables below show the full free-on-board prices for June in U.S. dollars.

Saudi term crude supplies to Asia are priced as a differential to the Oman/Dubai average:

data.title: Keisei Electric Railway Issues Bonds Totalling 10 Billion Yen
data.description:
Keisei Electric Railway is issuing its 64th series of unsecured straight bonds, totalling 10.0 billion yen, according to a Tokyo Stock Exchange filing on Wednesday.

Each bond has a denomination of 100 million yen, an interest rate of 1.852% per annum, and an issue and redemption price of 100 yen per face value.

The bonds have a nine-year redemption term, with the redemption date set for June 9, 2034, and interest paid semi-annually on June 10 and Dec. 10.

The offering period is on June 4, with a payment date of June 10, and the offering method is a public offering. These bonds are unsecured, unguaranteed, and have an A+ rating from Rating and Investment Information.

data.title: Soul Patts' Merger Brings Simplification Benefits — Market Talk
data.description: The simplified corporate structure created by the merger of Washington H. Soul Pattinson and Brickworks is seen as a net positive at Australian brokerage Morgans. Analyst Steven Sassine highlights the benefits of unpicking the companies' longstanding cross-holdings in each other. The melding of the investment manager and building-products maker further diversifies Soul Patts' portfolio and is likely to increase liquidity and the proportion of shares held by public investors. The latter is important for indexed and institutional funds, he reminds clients in a note. Morgans lifts its target price on Soul Patts by 15% to A$43.15 and keeps a hold rating on the stock, which is down 3.5% at A$39.95. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: State Gas Enters Agreement to Resume Operations at Queensland Project; Shares Rise 9%
data.description:
State Gas reached an agreement with the landowner at its Rolleston West Project in Queensland, allowing it to immediately resume operations, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company will now mobilize teams to drill two new exploration wells and start flow testing by the end of the month, the filing said.

Shares of the company rose roughly 9% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Japan 30-year bond auction bid-to-cover ratio 2.92, lowest since December 2023
data.description:
Demand at a sale of 30-year Japanese government bond yields saw the lowest demand since December 2023 on Thursday.

The bid-to-cover ratio, a measure of demand that gauges the number of bids against the amount of securities on offer, fell to 2.921 from 3.074 at the prior sale in May.

At the auction in December 2023, the ratio was 2.616.

Japan's Ministry of Finance announced the result of the 604.8 billion yen ($4.23 billion) sale at 0335 GMT.

Another gauge of demand called the tail - the difference between the lowest bid and the average bid - was 0.49 yen, compared with 0.30 yen at last month's auction. A longer tail signals less demand.

data.title: Indian stocks track Asian peers higher at open
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes opened higher on Thursday, mirroring gains in Asian peers, while Treasury yields and the U.S. dollar dipped.

The Nifty 50 rose 0.29% to 24,691.2, while the BSE Sensex added 0.24% to 81,196.08 as of 9:15 a.m. IST.

All 13 major sectors advanced at open. The broader, more domestically focussed smallcap and midcap stocks rose about 0.4% each.

The MSCI Asia ex-Japan index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was up 0.5%, led by Hong Kong stocks and South Korean shares, which hit an 11-month high on post-election optimism.

Overnight, Wall Street ended mixed, while Treasury yields and the dollar slipped, as data showed a contraction in the services sector for the first time in a year in May.

Back home, investors are waiting for the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) policy decision on Friday, when the domestic central bank is widely expected to cut key lending rates by 25 basis points for the third straight meeting.

data.title: A trader based in Vietnam offers Polypropylene (PP Film) in Sri Lanka
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Sri Lanka reported that a trader from Vietnam is heard to have offered its PP Film grade (China origin) at the USD 1030/mt levels.This offer is for July 2025 shipment on CIF Colombo port basis.

data.title: A trader based in Vietnam offers Linear low density polyethylene (LLDPE Film) in Sri Lanka
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Sri Lanka reported that a trader from Vietnam is heard to have offered its LLDPE Film grade (China origin) at the USD 995-1005/mt levels.This offer is for July 2025 shipment on CIF Colombo port basis.

data.title: Bookoff Reports 5% Existing Store Sales Growth in December 2024
data.description:
Bookoff Group Holdings reported a 3.7% year-on-year increase in net sales at directly operated existing stores in May, compared with a 3.3% sales increase across all stores.

The company opened two new stores during the month, one in Malaysia and another in Koto-ku, Tokyo.

Its total number of stores at the end of May 848, including 744 in Japan, 45 overseas, 53 premium services business and six 'other'.

data.title: Japan 30-year bond auction bid-to-cover ratio 2.92, lowest since December 2023
data.description:
Demand at a sale of 30-year Japanese government bond yields saw the lowest demand since December 2023 on Thursday.

The bid-to-cover ratio, a measure of demand that gauges the number of bids against the amount of securities on offer, fell to 2.921 from 3.074 at the prior sale in May.

At the auction in December 2023, the ratio was 2.616.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.2% Higher at 81196.08, Led by Auto, Tech Stocks
Saturn Metals Completes Metallurgical Test Work at Western Australia Gold Project; Shares Hit Three-Year High
data.description:
Saturn Metals said metallurgical test work using column leach testing on near-surface oxide and transitional material from its Apollo Hill gold project in Western Australia delivered gold recovery of 86% achieved in 95 days, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The test work was designed to confirm the suitability of the material for bulk tonnage gold mining and mineral processing using heap leach methods, the filing said.

The gold recovery across the full grade range is higher than the one used in the preliminary economic assessment for the project.

Saturn Metals' shares climbed over 9% in recent trading on Thursday, earlier reaching their highest point in three years.

data.title: AI arms race is only just getting started: podcast
data.description:
Follow on Apple or Spotify. Listen on the Reuters app. Read the episode transcript.

Listen to the podcast

Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon.com and Meta Platforms are set to spend $320 bln this year on data centres and other kit to power advanced chatbots. In this Viewsroom podcast, Breakingviews columnists explain why investors are willing to support a long game that will yield a return.

FURTHER READING

AI power demand is generating hallucinations

Microsoft’s AI future is trickier to price today

Capital intensity will reprogram Big Tech values

Visit the Thomson Reuters Privacy Statement for information on our privacy and data protection practices. You may also visit megaphone.fm/adchoices to opt-out of targeted advertising.

data.title: Elevate Uranium Defines Mineralized Envelope at Namibia Uranium Project
data.description:
Elevate Uranium defined a large mineralized envelope at the Namib IV tenement, part of the Koppies uranium project in Namibia, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The envelope is about 11 kilometers long and 7.5 kilometers wide. The current drilling phase is focused on determining the extent of mineralization, and the company said the results indicate that it could increase.

A total of 118 holes for 3,322 meters have been drilled since the March quarter.

The firm plans to conduct additional step-out drilling to define the extent of the mineralization, followed by infill drilling of selected areas to better define portions of higher-grade mineralization.

data.title: XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Likely to Benefit From Potential Post-Merger Cost Synergies — Market Talk
data.description: XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera stands to benefit from potential post-merger cost synergies, CGS International analysts say in a note. The merged entity of XL Axiata and Smartfren Telecom expects around US$100 million in synergies at pretax profit level in the first year of the merger, excluding integration costs, the analysts note. The Indonesian telecom company also aims to attain US$300 million-US$400 million in pretax synergies over the next 3-5 years. The brokerage upgrades the stock's rating to add from hold, as it believes the share price has yet to reflect potential post-merger cost synergies. It also raises the target price to IDR2,550.00 from IDR2,350.00 Shares are unchanged at IDR2,190.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's Lynas jumps after China rare earth export curbs raise supply fears for auto sector
data.description:
** Shares of Lynas Rare Earths rise as much as 11.8% to A$9.140, their highest level since February 8, 2023

** Lynas, which is the world's biggest producer of rare earth minerals outside China, extends gains after rising 3.3% on Wednesday

** China produces around 90% of the world's rare earths and has imposed restrictions requiring exporters to obtain licenses from Beijing

** LYC top gainer on ASX 200 benchmark index

** As of last close, LYC was up ~28% YTD

data.title: Market Chatter: SES Said to Secure Unconditional EU Antitrust Approval for Intelsat Acquisition
data.description:
SES is reportedly on course to obtain unconditional antitrust approval from the European Union for its potential $3.1 billion purchase of Intelsat.

The satellite communications provider plans to rival SpaceX's Starlink and Amazon's Project Kuiper through the proposed acquisition, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The EU is set to announce its decision on the deal by June 10.

SES and the European Union did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' requests for comments.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Materials Stocks Climb, Utilities Struggle
data.description:
The materials sector advanced 0.8% at midday Thursday as rare earth elements producers' stock prices rose after automakers warned that China's export controls could disrupt or halt production.

The US 10-year Treasury yield slid, and expectations increased that the US Federal Reserve will cut interest rates at least twice this year, after reports showed a contraction in the US non-manufacturing sectors and slow hiring across the private sector.

Nordic Nickel said it completed and settled the acquisition of the Kopsa, Kiimala Trend, and Hirsikangas gold projects in Finland via the issuance of 70 million ordinary shares in the firm to the vendor, Northgold, as well as a cash payment of 2 million Swedish kronor.

On the flip side, the utilities sector posted the biggest decline, falling 0.6%.

Meridian appointed Rory Blundell to the newly created role of general manager, strategy and portfolio, while Chris Ewers will take up a new position as electricity security manager.

data.title: Sichuan Kelun-Biotech falls most in 4 weeks on discounted share sale
data.description:
** Shares of Sichuan Kelun-Biotech Biopharmaceutical fall 6.4% to HK$336, on course for the biggest one-day drop since May 7

** Stock on track to snap two straight sessions of gains

** The oncology-focused drug developer plans sale of 5.92 mln new shares, or 2.5% of the enlarged share capital, in share placement raising HK$1.96 bln ($249.8 mln) for R&D, products commercialization and expanding product pipeline portfolio

** The new shares will be issued at HK$331.8 apiece, or 7.6% discount to Wednesday's close of HK$359 each

** Hang Seng Biotech Index slips 0.7%, while Hang Seng Index climbs 0.7%

** YTD, stock up 107.6%

($1 = 7.8452 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korean Banks Boost Bancassurance Sales Amid ELS Slowdown
data.description:
South Korea's five major banks are turning their focus away from equity-linked securities (ELS) to bancassurance products, fueling a sharp uptick in commission income, Pulse News reported Wednesday.

The five banks — Shinhan Financial Group's Shinhan Bank, Woori Financial Group's Woori Bank, KB Financial Group's KB Kookmin, Hana Financial Group's Hana Bank, and NH Nonghyup — earned 293.2 billion won from bancassurance sales through May, up 61% from a year earlier, the report said.

The shift comes as regulators ease sales cap rules, allowing greater flexibility in partnerships with insurers. This is driving bancassurance to become a key non-interest income stream, especially among high-net-worth clients who seek tax-efficient savings products, it said.

Shares of Shinhan Financial Group fell nearly 1% while those of KB Financial Group declined over 1% in recent trade on Thursday.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Malaysia's Consumer Sector Likely to Face Headwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's consumer sector is expected to post sequentially flattish earnings in 2Q, weighed by ongoing global uncertainties, reduced festive spending and subsidy cuts, says Apex Securities analyst Chelsea Chew says in a note. Trade uncertainty could lead to a potential economic slowdown, while the absence of major holidays, anticipated RON95 fuel-subsidy reforms and higher electricity tariffs could further dampen sentiment, she says. Apex Securities maintains a neutral rating on Malaysia's consumer sector, pegging Padini Holdings at buy as it believes the company is well-positioned to weather headwinds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: ST Engineering Likely to Offer Stability, Growth Potential — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore Technologies Engineering is likely to offer stability and growth potential, OCBC Investment Research writes in a note. Based on June 3 closing prices, the technology, defense and engineering group has delivered year-to-date returns of about 70% compared with the Straits Times Index's 5%, OCBC notes. ST Engineering's share price performance was also "super-charged" by positive sentiment relating to global defense plays and its solid fundamentals, OCBC says. There are also positive structural trends for the company's urban solutions & satcom segment over the longer term, it adds. OCBC raises its fair value estimate of the stock to S$8.54 from S$7.75, while maintaining a hold rating. Shares are at S$7.94. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: TeleChoice International Liquidates Dormant Chinese Unit
data.description:
TeleChoice International completed the voluntary liquidation of its indirect wholly-owned subsidiary, TeleChoice Technologies Shanghai, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The Chinese subsidiary had been dormant, and its liquidation is not expected to impact the company's earnings per share for the year ending Dec. 31.

data.title: Huahai Pharmaceutical Obtains Chinese Regulatory Approval for Tolvaptan Tablets
data.description:
Zhejiang Huahai Pharmaceutical received a drug registration certificate from China's medical products administrator for its Tolvaptan tablets.

The drug is used to treat hyponatremia and fluid retention caused by heart failure, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the pharmaceutical company rose more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Chinese Regulator to Review Hutchmed's New Drug Application for Renal Cell Carcinoma Treatment Combo
data.description:
China's National Medical Products Administration, or NMPA, accepted for review the new drug application for Hutchmed (China) and Innovent Biologics' fruquintinib-sintilimab combination.

The application is supported by data from the Frusica-2 study, which evaluated the combination as a second-line treatment for locally advanced or metastatic renal cell carcinoma, the pharmaceutical company said Thursday. The trial met the primary endpoint of progression-free survival and showed improvements in secondary endpoints such as objective response rate and duration of response.

The drug combination secured conditional approval in December 2024 from the regulator to treat patients with prior failed systemic therapy.

data.title: Ontario Pension May Call Final Bids This Month For India Hospital Asset, Sources Say
data.description:
By P.R. Venkat

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan seeks to solicit final bids this month for the Toronto-based investor's Indian hospital asset, people familiar with the process said.

Manipal Hospitals, supported by Singapore investment fund Temasek; a consortium including Malaysia-based IHH Healthcare; and a consortium led by private-equity firm Blackstone, are among those who have moved on to the next round to submit financial bids for the Ontario pension system's Sahyadri Hospitals, the people said.

The deal, if successful, could value the asset at up to US$650 million, one of the people said.

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, known as OTPP, declined to comment.

The Canadian investor acquired Sahyadri Hospitals from private-equity firm Everstone in 2022, joining a list of private-equity firms and strategic investors aggressively investing in the Indian healthcare sector.

A joint study published recently by Grant Thornton Bharat and the Association of Healthcare Providers India showed that from 2022 to 2024, India's healthcare and pharmaceutical sector recorded 594 merger-and-acquisition and private-equity transactions, totaling over US$30 billion in deal value.

Of those deals, hospital transactions accounted for nearly 40% of the total value. Transactions in the hospital sector alone in 2024 totaled US$6.1 billion in value.

Sahyadri Hospitals is one of the largest private hospital chains in the western Indian state of Maharashtra. It operates 11 hospitals with 1,300 beds and offers specialty services such as cardiac surgery, cardiology, and kidney and liver transplants.

OTPP managed net assets of 266.3 billion Canadian dollars, equivalent to about US$194.7 billion, as of the end of 2024. It invests in a variety of asset classes, ranging from public equity to fixed income, and from commodities to real estate.

Write to P.R. Venkat at venkat.pr@wsj.com

data.title: Wheat rises for 2nd session on Black Sea, China supply woes
data.description:
Key points:
  • Intensifying Russia-Ukraine conflict raises grain supply fears
  • Favourable US Midwest crop weather to cap corn, soybean prices

Chicago wheat futures rose on Thursday, supported by the escalating conflict between major grain suppliers Russia and Ukraine, as well as dry weather threatening yields in top consumer China.

Corn prices firmed, but soybeans edged lower.

"Ukraine has hit Russia and there are fears of escalation which is supporting wheat prices," said one trader in Singapore.

The most-active wheat contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) rose 0.6% to $5.46-1/4 a bushel, as of 0319 GMT. Corn added 0.3% to $4.40 a bushel, while soybeans gave up 0.1% to $10.44 a bushel.

Parts of China's wheat belt in Shaanxi and Henan provinces have been hit hard by hot and dry weather, with near-record temperatures threatening to reduce yields.

Ukraine has stepped up attacks on Russian targets, prompting Russian President Vladimir Putin to tell the U.S. President Donald Trump in a telephone conversation that Moscow would have to respond, escalating worries over grain supplies.

Ukrainian corn exports are set to fall to 1 million metric tons in June from 2 million tons in May, as Ukraine-origin corn is uncompetitive compared to its American equivalent, producers' union UAC said on Wednesday.

Corn and soybean prices are being capped by favourable weather in the U.S. Midwest, where farmers are wrapping up their planting.

Commodity funds were net buyers of CBOT corn, wheat, soybean and soymeal futures contracts on Wednesday and small net sellers of soyoil futures. (COMFUND/CBT)

data.title: GR Engineering Services Awarded Variation to Contract for Upgrade of Vault Minerals' Western Australia Processing Plant
data.description:
GR Engineering Services said it was awarded a variation to its existing engineering, procurement, and construction contract with Greenstone Resources (WA), a wholly-owned unit of Vault Minerals , for the stage two upgrade for the King of the Hills processing plant in Western Australia, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The value of the stage two upgrade variation is AU$79 million, including provisional sums, which brings the total contract value for the project to AU$155 million.

The scope of the project consists of the design, procurement, construction, installation, and commissioning of the upgrade to the King of the Hills plant, the filing said.

The stage two upgrade will increase the annual throughput of the wet processing plant to 7.5 million tonnes per annum.

GR Engineering's shares rose 1% in recent trading on Thursday, while those of Vault Minerals fell 1%.

data.title: Australia's Godolphin Resources soars on deal with Canada co
data.description:
** Godolphin Resources jumps as much as 20% to A$0.012; marks biggest intraday pct gain since March 18

** Stock hits highest since March 28

** Diversified miner inks earn-in and joint venture agreement with Canadian mineral explorer Great Plains Metals

** Says GPS will earn up to 51% stake in co's copper gold projects

** Godolphin Resources's managing director says deal will reduce co's expenditure commitments

** GRL last up 10%, trims YTD losses to 31%

data.title: Palm falls on weaker rival edible oils
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures slipped on Thursday, snapping a two-session rally, tracking weaker rival edible oils at Chicago and Dalian markets.

The benchmark palm oil contract for August delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange lost 51 ringgit, or 1.29%, to 3,897 ringgit ($919.97) a metric ton in early trade.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) fell 0.36%, while its palm oil contract declined 0.96%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were down 0.45%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* Malaysia's palm oil inventories are projected to climb for a third consecutive month in May, driven by a modest recovery in production despite robust export demand, a Reuters survey showed on Wednesday.

* India's palm oil imports in May surged to a six-month high, as lower inventories and the tropical oil's discount to rival soyoil and sunflower oil prompted refiners to increase purchases.

* Independent inspection company AmSpec Agri Malaysia said exports of Malaysian palm oil products for May rose 13.2%, while cargo surveyor Intertek Testing Services saw a 17.9% jump.

* Oil prices slipped in early trade on Thursday after a build in U.S. gasoline and diesel inventories and Saudi Arabia's cut to its July prices for Asian crude buyers.

* Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

MARKET NEWS

* Shares in Asia crept higher and the U.S. dollar languished ahead of the European Central Bank offering its policy outlook for a tumultuous global economy.

DATA/EVENTS

0145 China Caixin Services PMI May

0600 Germany Industrial Orders MM Apr

0600 Germany Manufacturing O/P Cur Price, Consumer Goods SA Apr

0830 US S&P GLOBAL PMI: MSC COMPOSITE - OUTPUT May

1230 US International Trade $ Apr

1230 US Initial Jobless Clm 31 May, w/e

1430 US EIA-Nat Gas Chg Bcf, Nat Gas-EIA Implied Flow 30 May, w/e

($1 = 4.2360 ringgit)
